ogTitle: Cotswold Hills vs Coulson: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cotswold Hills and Coulson.
heading: Cotswold Hills vs Coulson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cotswold Hills, Queensland and Coulson, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cotswold Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Coulson (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cotswold Hills or Coulson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cotswold Hills has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Coulson scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cotswold Hills scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Coulson scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cotswold Hills or Coulson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cotswold Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
